text: Perfumer. The Scripture reproaches this king alone with no fault. (Calmet)

Directed. Greek, "prosperous in converting the people," 4 Kings xxii., and 2 Paralipomenon xxxiv. (Haydock)<hr>He began when he was only eight years old: but set to work more effectually ten years afterwards. The prophets complain of the disorders which prevailed before that period. (Calmet)

Sinners. Or "sins," when iniquity was become fashionable. He consulted no false policy, (Haydock) but boldly exerted himself in God's service, and even invited the Israelites out of his dominions to come and celebrate the passover, 2 Paralipomenon 34:6. (Calmet)<hr>They belonged, in effect to him, as the twelve tribes were all promised to David's family, and the kingdom of Jeroboam was only a rebellion, Ecclesiasticus 47:23. (Haydock)

Sin. The two former effaced their sins by repentance, and never tolerated idolatry, which even Josaphat and Asa did, (3 Kings 22:43., etc.; Calmet) not destroying the high places. Many others yielded not to idol worship. (Worthington)
Despised. Greek, "they failed," and lost the crown.

They gave. Achaz called in to his assistance the king of Assyria, who afterwards claimed the kingdom as tributary to him, 4 Kings 18:7, 14. Greek, "He (God, Calmet; Alexandrian copy has They) gave their horn," etc. (Haydock)
Holiness. Jerusalem, which was styled holy, in the sicles of Simon.<hr>Desolate. No one came to the festivals, Lamentations 1:4.
Renew. To foretell the destruction and establishment of kingdoms, Jeremias 1:5, 10.

Rain. Denoting great misery, Ezechiel 13:11., and 38:22. (Calmet)<hr>And of. Literally, "to do good," (Haydock) or to announce the liberation of the penitent captives, Ezechiel 18:21., and 23:16., etc.
Place. Ecclesiasticus 45:15. The Pharisees adorned the tombs of the prophets, Matthew 23:29. Daniel is not mentioned among them, because he lived at court.
Hand. Most precious, Jeremias 22:24., and Canticle of Canticles 8:6.
Josedec. He was a noble figure of Christ, and helped to build the temple, Aggeus 1:2., and 2:3., and Zacharias 3:1., and 6:11.
Houses. He laboured much for the people's welfare, 2 Esdras v.
Henoch. Before the deluge. (Menochius)<hr>He stood up for the Lord, amid the most corrupt people, and was translated, Ecclesiasticus 44:16. He and Joseph have many advantages over the rest. (Calmet)
Joseph. He has not been named, but designated, Ecclesiasticus 44:27. (Calmet)<hr>Who. Greek, the "ruler," etc. (Haydock)

They prophesied. That is, by their being carried out of Egypt, they verified the prophetic prediction of Joseph, Genesis L. (Challoner) (Carriers.)<hr>This sentence is omitted in Greek. (Haydock)<hr>See Ecclesiasticus 48:14.<hr>Joseph had intimated that the Israelites should leave Egypt. (Worthington)

Men. The former was the father of the faithful, before the deluge; the latter after. (Calmet)<hr>Beginning. Greek, "in the creation" was appointed to rule. (Haydock)<hr>He alone among men was innocent, coming from the hands of God, and he might glory that he had no other father. (Calmet)<hr>Eve enjoyed the like privileges. (Haydock)
